Consumer complaint narrative:
I am submitting a complaint regarding Wells Fargo Bank for failing to post a government benefits ACH credit that the State of Florida Department of Economic Opportunity ( DEO ) confirmed was sent on XX/XX/year> in the amount of {$550.00}. 

The State provided a payment ID ( XXXX ) and confirmed the payment was issued and the disbursement file was transmitted. As of XX/XX/year>, the funds have not been credited to my Wells Fargo account. 

I contacted Wells Fargo multiple times by phone and in person at the branch. The branch manager, XXXX, and other employees stated they can not locate the deposit. However, the State confirmed the payment was successfully issued, which indicates the funds were sent to Wells Fargo but not posted to my account. 

Because of this delay, I incurred {$70.00} in overdraft fees and additional charges due to transactions attempting to clear based on deposits that historically post the next day. My benefits payments from this same agency have always posted the day after issuance. 

Wells Fargo informed me that a research request would take 10 days, and the branch manager stated he had never heard of an ACH trace, which raises concerns about whether Wells Fargo followed proper procedures for locating an unposted ACH credit. 

I am requesting the CFPBs assistance with the following : 1. Locating and posting the missing ACH credit 2. Reversal of the overdraft fees and any related charges 3. A full explanation of why the deposit was not posted 4. Review of whether Wells Fargo followed proper procedures for ACH investigations 5. Confirmation that government benefit payments are not being improperly delayed or withheld This delay has caused significant financial harm, especially because the missing deposit is a government benefits payment. I am requesting the CFPB investigate whether Wells Fargos actions constitute an unfair, deceptive, or abusive act or practice under federal law.
Company public response: Company has responded to the consumer and the CFPB and chooses not to provide a public response

Consumer complaint narrative:
Name : XXXX XXXX XXXX Check amount : {$1100.00} Bank : Wells Fargo Account type : Personal checking account Detailed Description of the Issue : On XX/XX/XXXX, I deposited my paycheck for {$1100.00} into my Wells Fargo account. Because I believed the deposit had not gone through, I mistakenly deposited the same check again at another bank. 

The issue is the following : On XX/XX/XXXX, Wells Fargo withdrew the amount of the check from my account, putting my account into a negative balance. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, Wells Fargo withdrew the same amount again for a second time. 
Neither bank credited the funds to my account, even though this money is my legitimate paycheck. 

As a result of these duplicate withdrawals, my account ended up with a negative balance of {$1700.00}, which was caused entirely by the banks errorsnot mine. 

Additionally, when I received my second paycheck, Wells Fargo automatically applied that deposit toward the negative balance they created. This means I also lost my second paycheck because the bank used it to cover an overdraft that was caused by their own mistake. 

At this point, I have gone over a month without receiving either of my paychecks, and my account continues to be affected by these errors. 

I have tried multiple times to resolve this issue directly with Wells Fargo : I have called customer service many times. 
My calls have been disconnected several times. 
I have three open cases, none of which have been resolved. 
I visited the branch in person, but they refused to assist or review my case. 

I have not received any formal explanation or solution from the bank. 



What I am requesting through the CFPB : 1. An immediate investigation into the duplicate withdrawal of the {$1100.00} check. 
2. That my missing paychecks be fully credited back to me, as these are my earned wages. 
3. That the negative balance of {$1700.00} be corrected and removed, since it was caused by the banks errors. 
4. That Wells Fargo return the funds from my second paycheck, which they took to cover an overdraft they themselves created. 
5. A formal written explanation from Wells Fargo regarding what happened and how the issue will be resolved. 

This situation has seriously affected my financial stability, and after more than 40 days of trying to resolve it with the bank, I have received no assistance.

Consumer complaint narrative:
CFPB COMPLAINT XXXX FRAUD, {$4000.00} LOSS & WELLS FARGO FAILURE TO INVESTIGATE Beginning in XX/XX/year>, approximately {$4000.00} in unauthorized XXXX transactions were charged to my Wells Fargo account. I did not make these purchases, authorize them, or receive any of the merchandise. 

When reviewing the XXXX order information, it showed that : The names, emails, and phone numbers tied to the orders are not mine. 

The merchandise was shipped to addresses I have no connection to. 

I have never lived at, used, or been associated with the delivery addresses. 

None of the items purchased were delivered to me. 


This clearly indicates third-party fraud using my financial information. 


-- - Impact on Me The total fraudulent activity amounts to approximately {$4000.00}, which has created significant and ongoing financial hardship. These losses have : Put me into a financial loophole, Set me back on essential bills and responsibilities, Created instability because the bank refuses to return the stolen funds. 


I have done everything required as a consumer to respond responsibly and promptly, yet the issue still remains unresolved. 


-- - Actions I Took to Report the Fraud XX/XX/year> Present : I notified Wells Fargo as soon as the unauthorized charges appeared. I opened multiple fraud claims and supplied all documentation, including : Screenshots and confirmation emails from XXXX XXXX details showing mismatched names and addresses Proof that I did not receive any items Communication logs with XXXX representatives The branch manager and district manager at Wells Fargo personally reviewed the materials and agreed that the activity appeared fraudulent. They escalated the case internally.

-- - Wells Fargo Executive Fraud Department Denials Despite all evidence, the Wells Fargo Executive Fraud Department : Denied my claim twice, Stated no fraud was found, Refused to consider the documentation showing mismatched delivery names and addresses, Declined to reopen the investigation, Would not communicate with XXXX to obtain the merchants fraud findings. 


The executive department has refused to budge or properly re-evaluate the evidence, leaving me without protection or reimbursement. 


-- - Communication With XXXX I contacted the XXXX XXXX Department, and after their internal investigation, they confirmed : The orders were not placed by me, The merchandise was shipped to unknown recipients, The contact information and delivery addresses do not match my identity, They have fraud findings on their end. 


However, XXXX has not forwarded their findings to Wells Fargo, which Wells Fargo claims is required in order to resolve the reimbursement. 

This has resulted in both companies pointing responsibility at the other, while I remain financially harmed. 


-- - Law Enforcement Involvement To further validate that this is fraud, I filed a report with the XXXX XXXX Police Department. Law enforcement agreed that the transactions appear fraudulent and documented the incident accordingly. This confirms an external party is using my financial information without permission. 

Despite a police report verifying the suspicious activity, Wells Fargos executive fraud department still refuses to take action. 


-- - Why I Am Requesting CFPB Intervention After nearly six months of efforts, the issue remains unresolved because : Wells Fargo closed my claims twice without proper investigation.

The executive fraud department refuses to revisit or analyze the supporting documentation. 

XXXX acknowledges fraud but has not communicated findings to the bank. 

I continue to suffer financial harm due to unauthorized charges totaling roughly {$4000.00}. 

All available evidenceincluding a police reportsupports that this is identity theft and fraud. 



-- - What I Am Asking the CFPB to Require 1. A full review of Wells Fargos mishandling of my fraud claims.

2. Immediate reimbursement of the {$4000.00} in unauthorized transactions, in compliance with Regulation E.

3. A requirement that Wells Fargo conduct a fair, complete investigation using the documentation I already provided.

4. That XXXX transmit their fraud findings to Wells Fargo so the bank can no longer deny responsibility. 


5. Assurance that my financial data is protected and that similar occurrences can not happen again.

I pay my monthly rent ( {$3600.00} ) with a check via mail -- the check is made out to the same company for the same dollar amount each month. Usually the check is cashed within the first week of the month, but this summer there was some delay with mail/depositing so some checks were cashed later in the month. I'd receive a " late notice '' via mail but by the time I received it the cash would have been checked and was no longer considered late. 

In XX/XX/XXXX, I renewed my lease and my rent increased to {$3800.00}. In XXXX, I spoke with my building management office about renewing the lease and they let me know that my last check ( XXXX ) was delayed in the mail but when it arrived they could see it was postmarked on time, therefore they weren't considering it late. When my XXXX check didn't post to my bank account within the first week of the month, I did not think anything of it because of prior delays. 

I spoke with the management office today ( XX/XX/XXXX ) because they mailed me a letter saying I had a late fee on my account, and I knew my checks had all been cashed since I last spoke to them ( so XXXX, XXXX & XXXX all showed as cashed on my Chase statement ). They said they never received it. After some back and forth I looked at the digital image of the check cashed on XX/XX/XXXX and saw that it was white-washed and someone had written their own name on the front of the check as the intended recipient. I wrote the check ( as I have every month since XX/XX/XXXX ) to my building management company, and they always use a stamp to endorse the back of the check. This check was tampered with and forged. The building management office told me to call my bank. 

When I spoke to my bank, they said it had been past the allotted time for me to report the fraud, and I " should have been more vigilant. '' The only information they were able to give me was that the fraudulent check was cashed at XXXX. My understanding is that XXXX would then be held responsible for reimbursement, as they cashed the fraudulent check. Chase would not open a claim and said the system was " built in a way that can not be overridden if it's been more than 65 days -- I reported it 7 days too late ). I was not asking Chase to reimburse me, I was asking them to document this as fraud so I could take a paper trail to the police. They told me I was out of luck and I should have looked to see who signed the check. Moving forward, I will do that, however, nothing seemed " off '' when I looked at my monthly transactions because it was a check cashed for the same amount I have paid every month for over a year. There were no flags. 

I then went to XXXX to get information on the account it was deposited into, so I could give the police everything as evidence. They told me they can't give me any information and that if I wanted info I would have to get the police to subpoena them. I can't get the police to do that because I can't get Chase to file a claim. 

I have never had this happen before and have been a loyal Chase client for years. I was notified of this transgression in my account today and reported it immediately. To be told I was " too late '' and " not vigilant '' is unacceptable. Neither Chase or XXXX is taking any accountability.

On XX/XX/year> I went to deposit {$500.00} in {$100.00} bills. I inserted my bank card and then it asked me what I wanted to do so I hit the deposit button. There was no note on the machine specifying that there was problems, but the associates inside the bank says that they were having trouble with it. Its been XXXX days and I have not heard anything. XXXX is around the corner. I am a single grandmother raising my grandson I would like my money in my bank account as I work very hard. XXXX told it will take 90 days as a consumer. I can not wait 90 days for my money. Thank you for your time.

I am submitting this letter to formally report serious concerns regarding Wells Fargos handling of a recent fraud incident involving my account and to request a full review of the banks actions. I am also providing this letter to my legal counsel for evaluation of my rights and potential next steps. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, a fraudulent transaction was posted to my Wells Fargo account. No merchandise was ever delivered because no merchandise was ever purchased by me. I immediately reported the transaction to Wells Fargos fraud department and complied with all required steps to dispute the charge. 

Despite the clear nature of the fraud, Wells Fargo subsequently denied the claim. During my follow-up call with the claims department, I was informed that I should take it up myself with the merchantdespite the fact that the transaction was not authorized by me, the merchant is fraudulent, and I have no legitimate business relationship with them. This response contradicts Wells Fargos publicly stated commitments regarding customer protection and zero liability for unauthorized transactions.

Wells Fargos own published policy states that the bank provides Zero Liability protection for unauthorized transactions on consumer accounts and that the bank will reimburse customers for confirmed fraud. The handling of my case does not align with these assurances, nor with industry-standard fraud-prevention practices that consumers reasonably rely on when using a major national bank. 

Furthermore, I am concerned that this situation may reflect broader systemic deficiencies in Wells Fargos fraud-prevention processes, online-banking protections, and claims-resolution practices. The failure to provide appropriate assistance, the directive to contact a fraudulent entity on my own, and the claim denial without reasonable justification collectively raise concerns about whether Wells Fargo is fulfilling its obligations under federal consumer-protection laws, including the Electronic Fund Transfer Act ( EFTA ) and related regulations. 

Given the volume of similar customer reports publicly available and the consistency of issues described, I am requesting that my legal counsel evaluate whether these practices may constitute a pattern of inadequate fraud-protection measures that could warrant further legal review, including the possibility of pursuing class-action remedies on behalf of affected customers.
Company public response: Company has responded to the consumer and the CFPB and chooses not to provide a public response

Consumer complaint narrative:
My name is XXXX XXXX. I opened a XXXX account in XXXX, and at that time I believed XXXX was withdrawing funds from my bank account to cover my XXXX copay insurance. However, I have now discovered that for seven years every single month I have been billed by XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX in the amount of {$470.00}. 

This totals over {$40000.00} withdrawn from my account without my knowledge or proper authorization. 

Last month, I changed my plan with XXXX specifically to prevent any further withdrawals related to XXXX. After doing this, I was shocked to see yet another withdrawal : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX {$470.00}. 

When I contacted XXXX today, I was told they never withdrew these funds not only this month, but at ANY time during the entire past seven years. They stated that NONE of the policies I had with them were set up for direct withdrawal from my bank account. 

I explained that the withdrawals were coming from XXXX XXXX XXXX, and XXXX responded that they do not use XXXX services, and I would need to contact XXXX. So I contacted XXXX and they had no record of my XXXX information, no account on file, no policy, nothing linked to me whatsoever. 

At this point, it is absolutely clear that for seven years my account has been drafted by a company or entity using the name XXXX XXXX XXXX, without any legitimate authorization. 

I contacted Wells Fargo and requested the merchant ID, transaction origin, and linked vendor account to determine where this money has actually been going. They refused to provide the merchant ID or trace the withdrawals, which prevents me from identifying the entity that has taken more than seven years of payments from me. 

I am extremely frustrated, and frankly, I feel completely victimized by a system that has allowed funds to be repeatedly withdrawn from my bank account with no verification, no authorization, and no accountability. I have all of the documented bank statements from Wells Fargo that show every withdrawal over the past seven years. 

I need urgent assistance in : obtaining the full merchant ID, identifying the true financial recipient, determining whether these withdrawals constitute fraud, and recovering more than {$40000.00} of payments that I never agreed to. 

This situation has gone on for far too long, and I will not accept being dismissed or told to go in circles between XXXX, XXXX, and Wells Fargo. I expect someone to take responsibility and provide a direct path to resolution immediately. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX

This situation has caused us severe financial stress, especially because these funds were needed to pay rent. Due to Bank of Americas mistake, we faced difficulties with our landlord and were at risk of being unable to pay rent on time.

1. Unblock my account,

I am filing a complaint because Bank of America has repeatedly placed improper and unlawful holds on deposited checks in violation of Regulation CC. These holds have included two Washington State Treasurer checks in XX/XX/year> and an earlier XXXX retirement distribution check. In one case, I experienced financial harm requiring a {$20000.00} 401k withdrawal. 

XXXX. XX/XX/year> State of Washington Treasurer Check {$19000.00} ( In-Person Deposit ) I deposited this check at a Bank of America branch . The bank placed a full hold until XX/XX/year>. The written notice stated only : We have information indicating the check ( s ) may be returned. 

This does NOT satisfy Regulation CC 229.13 ( e ), which requires a specific factual reason indicating collectibility concern. The notice uses impermissible boilerplate language ( document attached ) Additionally, Regulation CC 229.10 ( c ) ( 1 ) ( iv ) requires that at least {$5500.00} of a state or local government check deposited in person be available the next business day. The bank instead held the entire amount without justification. 

XXXX. XX/XX/year> State of Washington Treasurer Check {$5300.00} ( ATM Deposit ) The next day, I deposited another State of Washington check for {$5300.00} via ATM. Bank of America is this check until XX/XX/year> as well. 

I received no written notice, which is a violation of 229.13 ( g ) - ( h ). ATM deposits are still subject to the statutory next-day availability requirement for government checks when deposited into the payees own account ( 229.10 ( c ) and 229.19 ( a ) ).

Again, the bank failed to release the required next-day funds and did not provide a valid exception-hold reason. 

XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX / XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Check 27-Day Hold with No Notice I deposited a retirement distribution check ( drawn on XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/year>. The bank did not release the funds until XX/XX/year> 27 days later. 

Bank of America provided no written notice, despite written notice being mandatory under 229.13 ( g ) - ( h ). I was later told verbally that the hold was due to a minor misspelling of my middle name, which is not a lawful basis for an extended hold and does not constitute reasonable cause under 229.13 ( e ).

This delay forced me to take 401k withdrawal, so it could be wired into my account, causing financial harm. 

4. Pattern of Noncompliance Across all three checks, Bank of America : Held funds longer than permitted under Reg CC Used vague, boilerplate hold explanations Failed to provide legally required written notices Withheld required next-day availability amounts Applied full holds where partial availability is required Caused an overdraft cycle Caused material financial harm I request that the CFPB investigate these violations and require Bank of America to take corrective action.

In all three cases I spent lots of time going round and round on the phone, chat, or in person attempting to escalate, in all three cases their internal policies were referenced indicating these policies override federal statutes.

Consumer complaint narrative: Hi, I would like to report PNC Bank. I have disputed XXXX transactions XXXX for {$530.00}. They took my money back and messed up my account for the holidays. I need you to investigate and find out whats going on because I did not authorize this transaction and they took my money back from me.

On XX/XX/year>, multiple unauthorized debit card transactions totaling over {$2000.00} occurred on my Bank of America checking account. 

I had two physical debit cards linked to the same account : an original red Bank of America debit card and a XXXX XXXX Universitybranded debit card. 

- The two cards shared the same card number and expiration date but had different three-digit security codes ( CVV ) and functioned as separate physical cards. 

- The unauthorized transactions were made using the red debit card, which was not in my possession. 

- The XXXXbranded debit card remained in my possession and was used for legitimate transactions before and after the fraud. 

- I only disputed the unauthorized transactions made using the red debit card. 

- I reported the fraud on XX/XX/year>. 

- Bank of America issued a temporary credit on XX/XX/year>, then later froze my account and restricted access to my funds for over a month. 

- Bank of America denied the fraud claim, citing an accidental Yes response to a fraud alert and later undisputed transactions made using the XXXXbranded card. 

- Bank of America did not properly distinguish between the two physical debit cards in its analysis.

- Although access to my funds was later restored, the fraud claim denial remains unresolved.

- Under Regulation E, authorization must be evaluated by access device ; use of one physical debit card does not authorize transactions made on a different physical card that was not in the customers possession.

XX/XX/XXXX Citibank notified me that my account would be closed due to irregular activity. When I called, I was told the XXXX  check was flagged as fraudulent. I was not provided anything in writing explaining the fraud finding or the next steps.

XX/XX/XXXX XXXX  informed me that the check was returned by XXXX XXXX with REFER TO MAKER, and also told me the funds had already been captured by Citibank. 
XXXX XXXX Citibank confirmed by phone that the funds were received and would be mailed to me by check. This never occurred. 
XX/XX/XXXX Citibank informed me ( again by phone ) that the funds were being held until the issuing bank authorized release and that XXXX needed to call Citibank Fraud. I relayed this, but to date, the funds are still withheld.

Subject Formal Complaint Unauthorized Debit Card Issuance and Unauthorized Withdrawals ( Regulation XXXX Violation ) To Whom It May Concern, I am filing a formal complaint regarding Wells Fargos denial of my fraud claim involving unauthorized debit card issuance and unauthorized electronic fund transfers that occurred on XX/XX/scrub> 2025.

Under the Electronic Fund Transfer Act ( Regulation XXXX ), these transactions are unauthorized electronic fund transfers. I did not provide the card, PIN, or consent for these transactions, nor did I receive the card that Wells Fargo issued. Wells Fargo is legally required to conduct a reasonable investigation, including review of ATM records, teller logs, and branch security footage.
